meta.title: Reckoning Operationalization
meta.subtitle: 9 decisions from the project reckoning — institutional memory, orphaned initiatives, and trajectory

steps.title: Decision 1 of 9: Original Intent Restoration
steps.subtitle: The founding vision was replaced with a problem diagnosis

infoBox.html: Finding: The Original Intent — "Build a configuration layer for Claude Code that enforces engineering discipline" — was replaced with a diagnosis about enforcement-to-purpose ratio. The Original Intent section is described in the plan template as "the sacred text; the vision as first stated." The current text belongs in the Prompt Restoration initiative description, not in the project's north star.

specs.text: Recoverable from git show c2e0121:MASTER_PLAN.md
Original Intent is sacred — it's the north star for all future work
Current diagnosis text moves to Prompt Restoration initiative preamble
Captures how the project has grown beyond original scope
Acknowledges the religious/devotional framing that emerged
Loses the founding moment — Original Intent becomes a living document
Accurately describes the current challenge being addressed
Future planners will think this project was created to fix an enforcement ratio
Loses the founding vision that generated all prior work

steps.title: Decision 2 of 9: Decision Log Recovery
steps.subtitle: 72-73 decisions erased from an append-only log

infoBox.html: Finding: The Decision Log was reset from 77 entries to 4. Code contains 387 @decision annotations across 123 files, many referencing DEC-IDs absent from the plan. The log's own header says "Append-only record" — it was replaced, not appended. Traceability between plan and code is broken for 73 decisions.

specs.text: Recoverable from git show c2e0121:MASTER_PLAN.md
Restores plan-to-code traceability for all 387 annotations
Adds ~80 lines to the plan — manageable
Restore ~25 DEC-IDs that have matching @decision annotations in code
Smaller footprint — only what's actively referenced
Historical decisions without code references are still lost
Simple — no recovery work needed
387 code annotations point to nonexistent DEC-IDs
Principle 4 (Persistent Knowledge) remains violated

steps.title: Decision 3 of 9: Completed Initiatives Table
steps.subtitle: 12+ completed initiative summaries erased from the plan

infoBox.html: Finding: The Completed Initiatives table is empty. The plan's own template says "completed initiatives compress to ~5 lines and move to the Completed section — the plan is never discarded." At least 12 completed initiatives (Production Remediation, State Management, Hook Consolidation, Statusline IA, etc.) have no record in the current plan.

specs.text: Recover all completed initiative summaries from prior plan versions
5-line compressed summaries per plan template — minimal footprint
Future planners see the full project history at a glance
One row per initiative: name, period, key decisions — table only
Faster to produce, smaller footprint
Loses the narrative summaries that explain what was built
No work required
Future planners have no context about what the project accomplished
Violates plan template contract

steps.title: Decision 4 of 9: SQLite Unified State Store
steps.subtitle: Wave 1 merged with code, Waves 2-4 orphaned with open issues

infoBox.html: Finding: The SQLite initiative had 8 planning decisions (DEC-SQLITE-001 through 008). Wave 1 was completed — core API and unit tests are merged into main. Waves 2-4 have open GitHub issues #128-134. The initiative was silently removed from the plan. Merged code exists that the plan doesn't acknowledge.

specs.text: Wave 1 code already merged — foundation exists
SQLite WAL replaces fragile flat-file state with atomic CAS
3 more waves of work — significant commitment
Competes for attention with prompt restoration
Add to Parked Issues with clear explanation: complete after prompt restoration
Wave 1 code stays — it's stable and tested
Issues #128-134 stay open, tagged as parked
Formally acknowledges the deferral instead of silent removal
Clean break — no orphaned code or issues
Discards 8 planning decisions and completed Wave 1 work
Flat-file state remains with its known race conditions

steps.title: Decision 5 of 9: Operational Mode System
steps.subtitle: 9 decisions, 5 waves, deep-research validated — never implemented

infoBox.html: Finding: A 4-tier mode taxonomy (Observe/Amend/Patch/Build) with escalation engine, hook integration, and anti-gaming measures. 9 planning decisions, 5 waves of issues (#114-118), validated by deep-research. Never implemented; silently removed from plan. Potentially over-engineered for the problem space.

specs.text: Deep-research validated the concept
5 waves — largest initiative ever planned for this project
May be solving a problem that doesn't exist at current scale
Acknowledge the planning work — don't silently drop it
Revisit when the project has enough users to need mode control
Issues #114-118 stay open, tagged as parked
Cleanly removes scope that may never be needed
9 planning decisions become historical only
If mode control is needed later, planning starts fresh

steps.title: Decision 6 of 9: Backlog Auto-Capture
steps.subtitle: 5 decisions, never implemented — manual /backlog may suffice

infoBox.html: Finding: Automatic issue creation from conversation keywords. 5 planning decisions, never implemented. The manual /backlog command workflow exists and functions. Silent removal from plan without formal disposition.

specs.text: Auto-capture would reduce manual issue creation
prompt-submit.sh already detects deferred-work language
Additional complexity for a workflow that works manually
Keep the option open for later
Manual workflow handles current volume
Manual /backlog command works and is actively used
prompt-submit.sh already auto-captures deferred-work language
Closes scope that adds complexity without proportional value

steps.title: Decision 7 of 9: DEC-AUDIT-001 Collision
steps.subtitle: Same DEC-ID reused for a different decision

infoBox.html: Finding: The current plan has DEC-AUDIT-001 = "Governance signal map as markdown." The prior plan had DEC-AUDIT-001 = a different Hook Consolidation audit decision. Code annotations referencing the old DEC-AUDIT-001 now point to the wrong decision. This is a traceability collision.

specs.text: Current decision is newer — easier to renumber (only in plan + 1 code reference)
Historical DEC-AUDIT-001 stays consistent with existing code annotations
Simple find-and-replace in current plan and governance-signal-map.md
Current decision keeps its ID
Requires updating code annotations that reference the old ID
More code changes needed
Old → DEC-HOOKCON-AUDIT-001, New → DEC-GOVAUDIT-001
Prevents future collisions with initiative-namespaced IDs
Breaks all existing references to both — largest change

steps.title: Decision 8 of 9: Orphaned GitHub Issues
steps.subtitle: 75 open issues not tracked in any plan initiative

infoBox.html: Finding: 81 open GitHub issues exist. Only 6 are tracked in the current plan. 75 are orphaned — including SQLite Waves 2-4 (#128-134), Operational Mode System (#114-118), CI failures (#110-112), and numerous bug reports. Sacred Practice #9 says "Track in Issues, Not Files" but the issues aren't tracked in the plan either.

specs.text: Most thorough — each issue gets a proper disposition
Reconnects issues to plan initiatives or parks/closes them
Time-intensive — 75 issues to review
Can be done incrementally across sessions
Close issues older than 14 days with no activity
Park remaining under 'pending-triage' label
May close issues that represent real work (SQLite, OpMode)
Label all orphaned issues 'reckoning-triage', add to Parked Issues
Nothing is lost — but nothing is resolved either
Backlog continues growing

steps.title: Decision 9 of 9: Project Trajectory
steps.subtitle: Where should the project focus next?

infoBox.html: Finding: The project is bifurcated: the plan says "optimize prompts and audit governance" while the codebase says "we're also mid-migration to SQLite and have a planned mode system." The reckoning verdict is "drifting constructively" — genuine value but growing coherence gap. This decision sets the direction for the next 3-6 months.

options.title: Complete capability — finish what was started

specs.text: Finish SQLite migration (W2-4), build observatory learning loop
Fulfills Original Intent: "the observatory learns from traces to improve"
Prompt restoration pauses — current state is already improved
Risk: adding more before stabilizing what exists
Finish prompt restoration (W2-W3), complete governance audit
Reduce context injection noise, improve model output quality
SQLite and mode system stay parked
Risk: the plan-code gap continues growing silently

options.title: Strategic reset — fix the foundation first

specs.text: Address all reckoning findings: restore history, triage issues, fix collisions
Rebuild institutional memory so the plan reflects reality
THEN choose direction from accurate maps, not amnesia
Short pause on feature work — 1-2 sessions of cleanup
Risk: cleanup is less exciting than building, may lose momentum
